To Dismiss
Off Calendar Oct-28-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT OUAHIBA KHOURCHECH A/K/A WAHIBA KHARCHICH Motion To Dismiss

Defendant Ouahiba Khourchech brings a motion to dismiss the complaint for libel, slander, invasion of privacy, and intentional inflection of emotional distress. The court construes the motion under the demurrer statute (Code Civ. Proc., section 430.10) and under the special motion to strike or anti-SLAPP statute (id., section 425.16). Khourchech also objects to personal jurisdiction and venue. For the reasons stated herein, the motion is denied and Khourchech shall file an answer within 20 days of notice of entry of this order.

(see word minutes for complete ruling)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT OUAHIBA KHOURCHECH A/K/A WAHIBA KHARCHICH's Motion To Dismiss The Case.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Ouahiga Khourchech's motion to dismiss this case is ordered off calendar as untimely filed and served. A motion seeking relief must be filed with the court and served on all parties at least 16 court days in advance of the hearing date, with additional time if the motion is served other than by personal service. (See Code of Civil Procedure 1005(b).)

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Motion For Default Judgment Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-20-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Dept: 505; Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: K.Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" commencing at 9:48:43 a.m. pausing at 9:53:40 a.m., resuming at 10:27:17 a.m. pausing at 10:51:24 a.m. resuming at 11:05:42 a.m. and concluding at 11:19:10 a.m.(D505)

Re: Motion for Default Judgment

Appearances
Aleksandr Gruzman, Esq. representing Plaintiff Adil Ait Ouchraa who was also present via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Ouahiba Khourshech appeared in pro per. Defendant is assisted by Arabic Interpreter Nabil Abdelfattah; 300907.

The appearance of the defendant is made aware to the court.

Plaintiff counsel argues as to procedure in setting aside the default.

Defendant argues as to service of the lawsuit. Defendant states no service was done.

The matter of service is argued.

Defendant offers that there is a pending Federal Bureau of Investigation pending in relation to the matter but does not provide proof.

Plaintiff makes a record that Defendant had not done the proper procedure of setting default aside.

The Court orders Defendant to provide a mailing address and email address to the court where the defendant may be served.
The department shall send via electronic mail the information defendant shall provide.

The Court finds good cause to set aside the default against Defendant and provide opportunity for defendant to answer.
